arts.sum: Udinese have won 27 Serie A matches against Atalanta (D28, L25): only against Cagliari have the Friuliani collected more wins (28) in the top-flight. Atalanta have scored 29 goals in Serie A this season: only Bayern Munich (32) and Barcelona (40) have netted more in the big five European leagues in 2024/25.

arts.ttl: De Ketelaere sidelined for Atalanta’s clash with Udinese due to Injury
arts.sum: Atalanta has been dealt a blow ahead of their upcoming Serie A match against Udinese, as Charles De Ketelaere will be unavailable due to injury. The Belgian attacking midfielder has been a key player for Gian Piero Gasperini’s side this season, but will miss Sunday’s fixture due to a muscular problem. Corriere dello Sport report how De Ketelaere has suffered a lesion in his left biceps femoris, forcing him to sit out the weekend’s game. arts.ttl: Serie A | Atalanta v Udinese: Match Preview
arts.sum: The stakes are high for both teams, as Atalanta prepare to face Udinese in a crucial Serie A clash on Sunday. La Dea, buoyed by an impressive run of form, will look to extend their winning streak and solidify their position near the top of the table. Atalanta have been in remarkable form, securing four victories in just two weeks. Their recent triumph over Serie A leaders Napoli, where they clinched a convincing 3-0 victory at Stadio Maradona, has significantly boosted their confidence. arts.ttl: Atalanta’s Giorgio Scalvini set for comeback: Gasperini’s plans unfold
arts.sum: Atalanta has faced a challenging six months, marked by injuries within their defensive lineup while simultaneously securing a fourth consecutive Champions League qualification and celebrating a European Cup victory. The team’s defence has struggled, particularly at the beginning of the season when key players like Scalvini, Kolasinac, Djimsiti, Kossounou, Toloi, and Godfrey have all dealt with injuries. This situation forced coach Gian Piero Gasperini to make tactical adjustments, including repositioning players like Ruggeri and de Roon during critical matches. arts.ttl: Udinese Returnee Suffers Another Frustrating Injury Setback
arts.sum: Alexis Sanchez's return to Udinese has been a textbook definition of a nightmare, with the Chilean yet to make his long-awaited debut this season. arts.ttl: Sanchez’s Injury woes continue: New return date set for Udinese star
arts.sum: Alexis Sanchez’s return to Udinese has been anything but smooth, as the Chilean forward faces yet another setback in his recovery. After rejoining the club this summer, Sanchez has yet to step onto the pitch and is now dealing with new calf issues that have emerged during recent training sessions. According to reports from Calciomercato, the Udinese medical staff have recommended a precautionary break to ensure he regains full fitness and does not jeopardize his recovery progress. Sanchez will definitely miss the upcoming match against Atalanta, and his return may be pushed back even further. arts.ttl: Runjaic: ‘We don’t start defeated against Atalanta’
arts.sum: Udinese are seeking redemption after suffering two consecutive defeats as they prepare to face Atalanta away at Gewiss Stadium. Head coach Kosta Runjaic addressed the media in a press conference, emphasizing his team’s readiness to compete against a formidable opponent. Runjaic acknowledged the challenge posed by Atalanta, stating, “There are never easy matches; Atalanta is an absolutely valuable team. They played an extraordinary game against Stuttgart, showcasing principles and ideas with great discipline on the field. You can see the years of work behind them.
